overall: PARTIAL — some genuine signal, mostly artifact

corrected_analysis.note: Original Test D permutation null was flawed (shuffled pooled values instead of random-integer baseline). Density-corrected reanalysis below.

dim2.verdict: DENSITY ARTIFACT — NF discs cover 56% of integers in range

dim3.verdict: ANTI-CORRELATED — lattice dets AVOID NF discriminants at degree 3

dim4.verdict: GENUINE — 2.6x enrichment, p<0.001, overlap values are highly composite (125,144,256,432,...)
dim4.interpretation: Both dim-4 lattice dets and deg-4 NF discs are enriched for smooth numbers (small prime powers). Shared bias toward algebraically constrained integers, not random overlap.

C_interpretation.note: Within-signature overlap (47.5%) appears high vs Poisson null (6.5%), but this is because both datasets draw from the SAME finite pool of small integers with each signature. For primes: 16/17 lattice primes appear as NF discs, but expected is 17.0 because NF discs cover 99.9% of primes up to 10K.
C_interpretation.revised_verdict: SHARED SMALL-INTEGER BIAS — not prime artifact per se, but shared constraint toward small smooth numbers

corrected_summary.B_class_number: PRIME ARTIFACT (confirmed — class numbers are small integers)
corrected_summary.C_prime_detrending: SHARED SMALL-INTEGER BIAS (within-signature overlap is expected given finite pools)
corrected_summary.D_matched_dim2: DENSITY ARTIFACT (NF discs cover 56% of range)
corrected_summary.D_matched_dim3: ANTI-CORRELATED (lattice dets avoid NF disc values)
corrected_summary.overall: MOSTLY ARTIFACT with one genuine signal at dim=4: both 4-dimensional lattice determinants and degree-4 number field discriminants are enriched for highly composite numbers (125,144,256,432,...). This reflects shared algebraic constraints on 4-dimensional objects, NOT a deep structural bridge. The tensor bridge SV=5829 is inflated by shared concept labels (determinant, dimension_N, class_number) that map to trivially overlapping small-integer sets.
